entries.sourceExample: When something begins to exist or people start to become aware of it.
(not asleep) If you are not sleeping.
To say something about a person, thing, or state, to show a permanent or temporary quality, state, job, etc.
To accept, tolerate, or endure something, especially something unpleasant.
To defeat or do better than somebody. To hit repeatedly.
To start to be.
To start to happen or exist.
(make curved) To curve.
To gamble or risk something valuable against an unknown outcome.
To offer a particular amount of money for something that is for sale and compete against other people to buy it.
(tie, fasten securely) To tie something tightly or to fasten something.
To cut, wound, grip, hold or tear with the teeth.
To lose blood.
(air: move) To move and make currents of air, or to be moved or make something move on a current of air.
(1) To smash, split, or divide into parts violently; reduce to pieces or fragments. (2) To infringe, ignore, or act contrary to a law, rule, promise, etc.
(procreate) To keep animals for the purpose of producing young animals in a controlled way.
To carry, convey, conduct, or cause (someone or something) to come with, to, or toward the speaker.
To send out a programme on television or radio. To spread information to a lot of people.
To make something by putting bricks or other materials together. To create and develop something over a long period of time.
To be hurt, damaged, or destroyed by fire or extreme heat, or to cause this to happen.
The driver lost control when a tyre burst.
When you buy something, you pay money in order to own or obtain it.
To throw something. To choose actors to play particular parts in a play, film, or show.
To grasp something or seize it in your hands.
To decide what you want from two or more things or possibilities.
To tightly grasp something or to stick closely to something.
To move or travel towards the speaker or with the speaker.
If something costs an amount of money, you must pay that amount to buy or do it.
To move slowly, quietly, and carefully, usually in order to avoid being noticed.
To break the surface of something, or to divide or make something smaller, using a sharp tool, especially a knife.
(handle, manage) To do business with or be involved with someone or something. to give or share out somethin.
To break up and move soil using a tool, a machine, or your hands.
To act, perform, or undertake.
To make a picture of something or someone with a pencil or pen.
To experience events and images in your mind while you are sleeping.
To sip, slurp, or gulp a liquid.
To move or travel on land in a motor vehicle, especially as the person controlling the vehicle's movement.
To put or take food into the mouth, chew it (=crush it with the teeth), and swallow it.
To suddenly go down onto the ground or towards the ground without intending to or by accident.
To give them something to eat.
To experience something physical or emotional have a particular opinion about or attitude towards something.
To argue, to use physical force to try to defeat another person or group of people or to use a lot of effort to defeat or achieve something, or to stop something happening.
To discover something.
To be the right size or shape for someone or something. To be suitable for something.
To escape by running away, especially because of danger or fear.
To throw something or someone suddenly and with a lot of force.
To move through the air.
To refuse to allow something, especially officially, or to prevent a particular plan of action by making it impossible.
A statement of what is judged likely to happen in the future, especially in connection with a particular situation, or the expected weather conditions.
When you can't recall or remember something.
To stop blaming or being angry with someone, or not punish them for something.
(abandon) To leave someone entirely, usually in a moment of need.
If you freeze something, you lower its temperature below 0°C.
To obtain, buy, or earn something. To receive or be given something. To go somewhere and bring back someone or something. To take someone or something into your possession by force...*
To hand over possession of something to someone else.
To proceed or move from one place to another.
To make something into small pieces or a powder by pressing between hard surfaces. To rub something against a hard surface, in order to make it sharper or smoother.
To increase in size or amount, or to become more advanced or developed.
To fasten or support something at the top leaving the other parts free to move, or to be held in this way.
To possess something.
To perceive a noise made by someone or something.
To prevent something from being seen.
To smack or strike someone either with an object or with your hand.
To take and keep something in your hand or arms. To contain or be able to contain something.
To feel pain in a part of your body, or to injure someone or cause them pain. To cause emotional pain to someone. To cause harm or difficulty.
To have and hold on to something.
To go down into, or stay in, a position where one or both knees are on the ground.
To have a piece of information or a certain understanding of something.
To put something down.
To go in front, or to serve as the leader of a group.
To (cause to) slope in one direction, or to move the top part of the body in a particular direction.
(jump) To make a large jump or sudden movement, usually from one place to another.
To gain new knowledge or skills through study, practice or experience.
To go away or exit.
To give something to someone for a short period of time, expecting it to be given back.
To allow something to happen or someone to do something by not doing anything to stop an action or by giving your permission.
He lay on the bed.
To produce light that makes an object or area bright or easy to see. To start to burn or to make something start to burn.
To no longer have something because you do not know where it is. To have something or someone taken away from you. To stop feeling something. To fail to succeed in a game, competition etc.
To produce something, often using a particular substance or material. To cause something.
To intend, express or represent something such as an idea, thought, or fact.
To interact in some way.
To lose something temporarily by forgetting where you have put it.
To do something incorrectly.
To give money in return for something.
To show that something is true, to show a particular result after a period of time.
To move something or someone into the stated place, position, or direction.
To stop doing something or leave a job or a place.
To scan and understand letters and words, making sense of language.
When you clear out or eliminate something you don't want.
He was riding on his horse looking for the castle.
To make a phone call to someone:to make a phone call to someone.
To move upwards. To stand, especially after sitting. To increase. To become important, successful, or powerful.*
To move along quickly, and for a long time.
To get an opportunity to deliver your opinion on something.
Is what your eyes do.
To try to find or get something, especially something that is not a physical object. To ask for advice, help, approval, permission, etc. To try or attempt.
To exchange a thing or a service for money.
To cause something to go from one place to another, especially by post or email.
To set something is to put it somewhere.
To move backwards and forwards or up and down in quick, short movements, or to make something or someone do this.
To lose a covering, such as leaves, hair, or skin, because it falls off naturally, or to drop something in a natural way or by accident. To get rid of something you do not need or want.
(direct light) To give off a bright, glowing light.
To fire a bullet or an arrow, or to hit, injure, or kill a person or animal by firing a bullet or arrow at him, her, or it.
To display something, to share it.
To become smaller, or to make something smaller.
To close something or stop operating or being in service, either temporarily or permanently.
To make music with your voice.
When something settle or drop to a lower level.
To be in a position in which the lower part of the body is resting on a seat or other type of support, with the upper part of the body vertical.
The period of sleeping, resting state in which the body is not active and the mind is unconscious.
The canoe seemed to slide painfully on a mirror (of a lake).
To make a long, straight, narrow cut in something.
To have the ability to notice or discover a substance in the air by using your nose.
(move silently) To go somewhere secretly, or to take someone or something somewhere secretly.
Do you speak English?
To move, go, or happen fast.
To form a word by writing or naming the letters in order.
To give money as a payment for something, to use time, energy, effort, force etc., doing something or being somewhere especially until there is no more left.
To cause something to flow, move, fall, or spread over the edge or outside the limits of something.
To cause something to turn around and around, especially fast.
To force out the contents of the mouth, especially saliva.
To divide into two or more parts, especially along a particular line.
To destroy something or ruin its quality.
(expand) To cover, reach, or have an effect on a wider or increasing area.
To be wound inflicted by a bee or hornet, or to a kind of burning pain.
To clean especially a floor by using a brush to collect the dirt into one place from which it can be removed.
(propel through the air) To send something through the air with force, especially by a sudden movement of the arm.
(cry) To express sadness that is more profound.
To put pen to paper or fingers to keyboard.
(not tolerate) To abide by something, like a rule, means to obey it.
If something bad or dangerous befalls you, it happens to you.
(see, observe) To see or look at someone or something.
To live in a place or in a particular way.
To be able to.
To twist together or combine two or more things so that they cannot be separated easily.
(predict) To say what is going to happen in the future.
To sleep for longer than you intended to and so wake up late.
To hear what other people are saying without intending to and without their knowledge.
(tear, rip) To tear or break something violently.
(informal) To throw, give or drop something carelessly.
(move furtively) To walk away from somewhere quietly so that you are not noticed.
To leap or bounce up suddenly.
To be in a vertical state or to put into a vertical state, by making the legs straight.
To take something without the permission or knowledge of the owner and keep it.
To cause something to become fixed, for example with glue or another similar substance or tool.
To smell very unpleasant.
To walk somewhere quickly with long steps.
He struck the ball straight into the hospitality tents.
To put strings on a musical instrument.
To endeavor, reach, or strain for something above or beyond.
To promise or say firmly that you are telling the truth or that you will do something or behave in a particular way.
When you move yourself through the water using your arms and legs.
To sway back and forth, or to move in a sweeping, curving motion.
To gain possession of or lay hold of something.
To transfer ideas or skills to another person.
To pull or be pulled apart, or to pull pieces off.
To describe or announce something, either by speaking or writing.
To have an idea, belief, or thought about something.
To put your foot on something or to press something down with your foot.
To push forward quickly and forcibly.
To comprehend or get it.
(project: commit to) To promise, do or begin to do something, especially something that will take a long time or be difficult.
To unfasten something that is fastened or tied. To remove the good or bad effects of an action or several actions.
To be disturbed or very unhappy.
To cause someone to become awake and conscious after sleeping.
To clothe, or be covered by them.
To achieve first position and/or get a prize in a competition, election, fight, etc.
To take or move out or back, or to remove. To take money out of an account.
(resist) To be strong enough, or not be changed by something, or to oppose a person or thing successfully.
To twist or squeeze it, usually out of its original shape.
To jump head first into the water.
To make cloth by repeatedly crossing a single thread through two sets of long threads on a loom.
To cause it to happen or be created. To ber the father of someone.
